_provenance: Schema-derived fixture with hand-computed Rockafellar-Uryasev reference values. pool_distinct_returns is a fully-specified synthetic pool where every entry was chosen manually (not by running the SUT). Reference CVaR was computed via closed-form R-U general-distribution formula (Rockafellar & Uryasev 2002) independently of compute_portfolio_cvar or compute_cvar_5pct_general_distribution. See m2_cvar_known_pool.derivation.md for derivation steps. Conforms to D-2 non-circular provenance requirement.

_notes.pool_design: N=150 entries (MC_DEFAULT_NEIGHBOR_K). First 7 values strictly below VaR, 8th value is the atom AT the alpha-quantile (alpha*N=7.5, so fractional_weight=0.5 and atom contributes half-weight). Remaining 142 values above VaR. This construction ensures the naive 'mean of k below-VaR' estimator differs from the correct R-U value, making the fixture discriminating for A-1.
_notes.atom_handling: sorted_pool[7] = -0.01 is the alpha-quantile (5th percentile of 150 values). R-U formula: CVaR = (1/alpha)*(1/N)*(sum_below + fractional_weight*atom) = (1/0.05)*(1/150)*(-0.192 + 0.5*(-0.01)) = 20*(1/150)*(-0.197) = -0.026267. The naive estimator (mean of the 7 below-VaR values) gives -0.027429, a 4% difference. When atom handling is wrong this test fails.
_notes.H2_stderr_design: n_tail_distinct=8 (the 7 below-VaR + 1 atom = the genuine sub-5% distinct observations). stderr_correct = std(tail_values, ddof=1)/sqrt(8) = 0.004988. stderr_wrong_resample = std(tail_values, ddof=1)/sqrt(5000) = 0.0001995. Ratio = sqrt(5000/8) = 25.0x. A 5% tolerance band around stderr_correct (0.004988) does not include stderr_wrong_resample (0.0001995), so the wrong-resample negative assertion with 50% band safely separates them.
_notes.S3_contract: The four S-3 display elements are recorded as load-bearing strings: (a) stderr value, (b) tail_obs_count=8, (c) 'diagnostic, not a signal - do not trade on this', (d) 'known-low-biased LOWER BOUND on tail severity, not a point estimate'.

pool_tail_5pct._note: 8 = 7 values strictly below VaR (-0.01) + 1 atom AT the VaR. The atom contributes with fractional_weight=0.5 (alpha*N=7.5, k=7, fractional_weight=7.5-7=0.5). n_tail_distinct=8 is the DISTINCT GENUINE TAIL count for the H-2 stderr denominator.

expected._var_derivation: sorted_pool[k] where k=floor(0.05*150)=7 (0-indexed). The 8th-smallest value is -0.01.
expected._cvar_derivation: R-U formula: (1/alpha)*(1/N)*(sum_below + fractional_weight*var). sum_below=sum([-0.05,-0.04,-0.03,-0.025,-0.02,-0.015,-0.012])=-0.192. fractional_weight=7.5-7=0.5. CVaR=(1/0.05)*(1/150)*(-0.192+0.5*(-0.01))=20*(1/150)*(-0.197)=-0.026267. Naive mean of k=7 below-VaR values: -0.02743 — differs by ~4%, confirming atom-handling discriminating power.
expected._stderr_correct_derivation: std(tail_values, ddof=1)/sqrt(n_tail_distinct=8). tail_values=8 values. std(ddof=1)=0.014124. stderr=0.014124/sqrt(8)=0.004988.
expected._stderr_wrong_derivation: std(tail_values, ddof=1)/sqrt(5000). stderr=0.014124/sqrt(5000)=0.0001995. This is the forbidden value: using the resample count understates the true stderr by sqrt(5000/8)=25x.

discriminating_power: stderr_correct / stderr_wrong_resample = sqrt(5000/8) = 25.0x. A 5% tolerance band around stderr_correct does not include stderr_wrong_resample (separated by 25x). A 50%-band negative assertion safely rejects the wrong-resample value.

s3_display_contract.bias_warning_substring: known-low-biased LOWER BOUND on tail severity, not a point estimate
